app.description: Create a house-standard Angular app (the application sibling of publishable-lib). Delegates to @nx/angular:application with the house defaults (minimal/scss/routing/no-e2e), then applies the per-app house config the raw generator omits: the unified `serve` target + `dev-server` leaf (via the serve generator), serve host 0.0.0.0 (via serve-options), and — when the workspace is a Firebase workspace — the full per-app Firebase wiring (env files, firebase.config.ts, app.config wiring, prod fileReplacements, platform:web tag, emulator suite; via firebase-emulators). Firebase is self-detecting (explicit --firebase wins; else inferred from firebase.json at the workspace root), and the emulators' demo-<workspaceName> identity is resolved once from the workspace root, never the app name. Single source of truth for adding ANY app to a BeSpunky workspace — the scaffolder's first app and every later app run through this one generator; never reach for the raw @nx/angular:application generator, which leaves the per-app config out.

serve-options.description: Set BeSpunky house dev-server options (host 0.0.0.0) on a project's dev-server target — the `dev-server` leaf created by the `serve` generator (legacy dev-server names tolerated for --repair). Strips any stray `host` off the composing `serve` target so it isn't forwarded as a flag.

serve.description: Wire the unified house dev loop onto a project: a `dev-server` leaf (@angular/build:dev-server — host 0.0.0.0, configurations development (default) / production, buildTarget <app>:build) and the `serve` composing target (@bespunky/nx-tools:serve — dev-server + optional Firebase emulators + optional shared co-driven browser, for the current or a chosen git worktree, under one graceful Ctrl+C). Replaces the old serve/serve-worktree/serve-with-shared-browser trio — the worktree and shared-browser axes are now flags (--worktree, --portOffset, --no-emulators, --no-shared-browser). Also wires the dev-only worktree tab label (worktree-tab-label.ts + app.config provider): on a `<slug>.localhost` domain it prefixes the tab title with `[slug]` and tints the favicon, tree-shaken from prod via ngDevMode. The per-app sibling of serve-options — applied by the house `app` generator to every app (first and later) and re-asserted by `scaffold.sh --repair`. Idempotent + self-healing (consolidates the raw @nx/angular `serve` and legacy dev-server names into the `dev-server` leaf).

devcontainer.description: Generate the BeSpunky-standard .devcontainer/devcontainer.json (Claude CLI + extension, .claude persistence, plugin pre-install).

claude-settings.description: Generate .claude/settings.json wiring the claude-toolkit + nx marketplaces (autoUpdate on); keep .claude/data out of git.

angular-ai.description: Wire Angular's official AI tooling into the project. Writes project-scoped .mcp.json declaring the Angular CLI MCP server (`npx -y @angular/cli mcp`, always-latest) with its DEFAULT knowledge tools (get_best_practices, search_documentation, find_examples, ai_tutor, onpush_zoneless_migration); the experimental exec tools (build/devserver/test/e2e) are deliberately omitted because an integrated Nx workspace has no root angular.json and Nx owns those targets. Merges into any existing .mcp.json (preserves user-added servers). The Angular agent skills (angular-developer, angular-new-app) are NOT vendored here — post-create.sh fetches them fresh from github.com/angular/skills into .claude/skills/ on every container build; this generator just keeps that cache out of git.

firebase-emulators.description: Scaffold Firebase emulator config + Nx targets + app initialization using Angular's canonical environment-files pattern. Writes firebase.json (emulators block + singleProjectMode); deliberately does NOT write .firebaserc (cloud-project linkage is the Firebase CLI's job — `firebase use --add`). Generates src/environments/{environment.interface.ts, environment.ts, environment.prod.ts} (the latter two only if absent — real web config preserved), where environment.ts carries a PER-SERVICE emulator toggle (the EMULATE map → each service's `default`). Writes src/app/emulator-overrides.ts (generator-owned: the per-session ?emulate=/?real=/localStorage resolver, precedence default<localStorage<URL — resolving ?emulate=none → every service real) and src/app/firebase.config.ts (when absent or on a legacy shape) which gates EACH service independently on committed-default ⊕ override (so e.g. Firestore emulated + real Auth). Registers the production build configuration (→environment.prod.ts); wires app.config.ts, adds the workspace `firebase:emulators*` targets (each passes --project=demo-<name> so emulators work without login). The app's dev-server is the single `dev-server` leaf (created by the serve generator); firebase-emulators adds only the emulator suite + app-side firebase wiring. --repair self-heals stale environment.no-emulators.ts, build:no-emulators, and the serve-with-emulators/serve-no-emulators targets.

playwright.description: Add @playwright/test as a devDependency so the devcontainer's post-create.sh installs Chromium + apt deps on container build. The browser binary lives in a per-workspace Docker volume so rebuilds don't re-download. Pairs with the `bespunky-browser-automation:playwright` skill which describes how to use it. Always-on for BeSpunky projects — no opt-in flag.

shared-browser.description: Install the always-on shared, co-driven browser tooling — the workspace-level sibling of firebase-emulators' tooling. Writes the shared-browser CLI (tools/shared-browser/shared-browser, chmod +x) and its Node/CDP helpers (attach.mjs, verify.mjs, recorder.mjs) into tools/shared-browser/, and registers the workspace Nx project `shared-browser` with the lifecycle targets up|down|status|restart|clean|url|logs (each an nx:run-commands running `bash tools/shared-browser/shared-browser <verb>`). The shared browser is ONE headed Chromium on a virtual display (Xvfb), co-driven by a human over noVNC (:6080, the only forwarded port) and by an agent over CDP (:9223, loopback), so a change can be verified live in the same browser both watch. Workspace-level and always-on (no inputs) — generated once per workspace from scaffold.sh's WORKSPACE_GEN_BLOCK in both the scaffold and --repair paths. Idempotent + --repair-safe: every generator-owned file is rewritten on each run. Per-app consumption is the `serve` target's shared-browser layer (wired by the `serve` generator).

worktree-domains.description: Install the workspace-level worktree-domains proxy + route registry (the sibling of shared-browser). Writes the CLI (tools/worktree-domains/worktree-domains, chmod +x) and its self-contained Node reverse proxy (proxy.mjs — Node built-ins only, no npm/apt dep), and registers the workspace Nx project `worktree-domains` with the housekeeping targets list|reconcile|status|logs|stop. One tiny proxy on 127.0.0.1:80 (loopback, container-internal, NOT forwarded) fronts every worktree's dev-server: a browser hitting http://<slug>.localhost/ (Chromium auto-resolves any *.localhost to loopback — no DNS/hosts) is routed by the leading DNS label to 127.0.0.1:<port> per the slug→port registry, and WebSocket upgrades are proxied so Angular/Vite HMR survives. The `serve` executor drives it per-serve via `worktree-domains register <slug> <port>` / `unregister <slug>` (idempotent; starts the proxy if down). Workspace-level and always-on — generated once per workspace from scaffold.sh's WORKSPACE_GEN_BLOCK. Idempotent + --repair-safe: every generator-owned file is rewritten on each run.

house-doc.description: Write the generated HOUSE.md — the toolkit-owned house reference (architecture directives, branch/release workflow, serving, worktrees, Firebase, Nx, shared browser, Playwright), rewritten every run to match the installed @bespunky/nx-tools — and keep a bounded, marker-delimited pointer to it in CLAUDE.md (the only part of CLAUDE.md it touches). Moves the volatile house guidance OUT of the hand-owned CLAUDE.md (where --repair can't reach it and it went stale) into a repairable doc. Workspace-level, always-on; idempotent (HOUSE.md fully rewritten; the pointer upserted between its markers).

mark-extractable.description: Mark an existing Nx library as a reusable-tool extraction candidate: add the `reusable-tool:extraction-candidate` tag and write an extraction.json marker (proposed @bespunky package name, kind, declared deps, and installed framework version ranges) so the host-side extract-tool can lift it into the shared workspace later. Sandbox-safe (touches only this workspace). PREREQUISITE: structure the generic code into its own clean Nx library first — this marks it, it doesn't abstract it.

adopt-extracted.description: Close the extraction loop: replace a local library (already published as @bespunky/<name>) with the public-npm package. Honours verify-then-delete — the default run adds the package + rewrites imports but keeps the lib; build to verify, then re-run with --finalize to remove the now-unused local lib. --keepShim instead leaves the lib as a thin re-export for staged migration.

navigation-core.description: Scaffold the self-contained navigation-core kernel — the reusable base that domain-specific navigation builds on (the bespunky-engineering:typed-reactive-navigation pattern). VENDORS navigation-x verbatim (@bespunky/angular-zen/router-x/navigation): routeConfigFor / provideRouterX / useNavigationX / RouteComposer / strong Router typing (the auto-derived typed navigator). On top, BeSpunky reusable additions navigation-x doesn't yet ship: the BsNavLink directive that AUGMENTS Angular RouterLink via hostDirectives (inherits the real href + every RouterLink feature — queryParams/fragment/target/relative/RouterLinkActive — and adds only a typed composer binding + a `navigated` telemetry seam; it does not reimplement RouterLink), useNavigationLinks (the composer-value twin of useNavigationX, for binding links), RouteState (URL read-side signals), a typed EventBus + bindEvents, and navigation middleware. Files are copied byte-for-byte (no tokens, no formatting) to stay faithful to upstream; treat lib/* except extras/ as vendored. Adds @bespunky/typescript-utils (the kernel's type-util dependency). Run once per workspace: `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:navigation-core [--directory=...]` (default libs/navigation-core/src).

domain-navigation.description: Scaffold a domain's THIN navigation config — the per-domain layer of the bespunky-engineering:typed-reactive-navigation pattern, on top of the navigation-core kernel (run `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:navigation-core` once first). Emits ONLY what is domain-specific: the typed route tree (routeConfigFor<Entity>().route(... as const)), the entity + event union + a domain EventBus<Event> subclass, the navigation file (useNavigationX/useNavigationLinks wrappers + the PURE event->navigator mapper + binding + provideX), and domain selectors extending RouteState. The composer, auto-derived navigator, the BsNavLink directive (real href so copy-link/open-in-new-tab survive), the event bus and the selectors base are INHERITED from navigation-core — never regenerated. After running, point the `@navigation-core` import at your lib (path alias or relative). Run as `nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:domain-navigation <domain> [--directory=...]`; files land in <directory|libs/<name>/src/lib>/navigation/.

publishable-lib.description: Scaffold a BeSpunky-standard publishable library. Delegates to @nx/angular:library (or @nx/js:library when --nonAngular) then post-processes to the house standard: ng-packagr FESM2022 build to dist/{projectRoot}, Vitest unit tests (unitTestRunner vitest-angular), a per-project `nx release` block (version.currentVersionResolver git-tag + fallbackCurrentVersionResolver disk) and nx-release-publish.packageRoot dist/{projectRoot}, modern nested ng-package.json (entryFile src/index.ts, NO umdModuleIds), standalone-only (skipModule), prefix bs. LINKING MODEL: in-repo resolution is the tsconfig.base.json PATH ALIAS the base generator writes (via addPathMapping) — NOT package-manager workspaces (`workspace:*`) and NOT TS project references. Cross-lib deps (--workspaceDeps) are declared on the lib's own package.json as REAL caret ranges (`^<sibling's current version>`) purely as the published-consumer contract, and Angular libs also list them in ng-package.json `allowedNonPeerDependencies` (ng-packagr hard-fails otherwise). Test-only peers (vitest) are marked optional so consumers don't get bogus unmet-peer warnings. --nonAngular switches to a plain-TS @nx/js:tsc library with NO ng-package.json (typescript-utils/rxjs). Never touches nx.json (root release/releaseTag config is Foundation's job).

secondary-entrypoint.description: Add a secondary entry point to a publishable library: <lib-root>/<name>/{ng-package.json (entryFile src/index.ts), src/index.ts}, which ng-packagr auto-discovers by filesystem scan so it becomes the `<package>/<name>` deep-import subpath with no extra registration. In-repo, the deep import resolves through the tsconfig.base.json PATH ALIAS the delegated @nx/angular generator writes — that alias is the only in-repo resolution channel on the paths linking model, so it is deliberately KEPT. Generalized from xpand's design-system-secondary-entrypoint: delegates to @nx/angular's librarySecondaryEntryPointGenerator (skipModule), normalizes the nested ng-package.json, and RESETS the parent lib's tsconfig.lib.json include/exclude to a bounded, fixed-size glob pair (the delegated generator appends cumulatively, and N entries explode into a multi-MB tsconfig whose globs compile past V8's regex nesting limit — TS500 — killing the ng-packagr build). --component=standalone optionally scaffolds a standalone component and re-exports it; default --component=none leaves an empty src/index.ts the consumer fills. Standalone-only (no scam). For a DESIGN-SYSTEM component, use the `ds-component` generator, which composes this one and adds the sass channel + the house token-reading SCSS.

design-system.description: Create the workspace's DESIGN SYSTEM — the single source of visual truth every BeSpunky project is born with, because the alternative is that feature one typed a hex into a component, feature two copied it, and by screen five there is no system left to retrofit. Composes `publishable-lib` (so: a real publishable library — ng-packagr, `nx release` git-tag baseline, Vitest, the tsconfig path alias — at packages/design-system, tagged `type:design-system`, with an import path derived from the WORKSPACE's scope, never @bespunky). Ships the MECHANISM and nothing else: design tokens as CSS CUSTOM PROPERTIES (the runtime layer — a theme change is a re-binding through the cascade: live, no rebuild, no second stylesheet), a ZERO-OUTPUT SASS API (the author-time layer: token accessors that @error on an unknown key, the one-and-only theme() emission, mixins, placeholders — with a PUBLIC barrel (`styles/_index.scss`, an explicit `@forward … show` contract) and the implementation in `_`-prefixed folders named for WHAT THEY ARE — `styles/_core/` (the token engine) and `styles/_utils/` (the authoring toolkit), never a folder called `internal`), the TS half of the theming mechanism (DsTheme — the one thing that writes the `[data-ds-mode]` attribute, light/dark/system, SSR-safe — plus DsRuntimeTheme, which SWAPS themes at runtime — a theme being a standalone CSS FILE of token overrides authored in SASS via `ds.theme-overrides()` and generated by `ds-theme`, linked so it applies BEFORE first paint and keeping the compile-time unknown-token guard; JS token overrides exist only for values that don't exist until runtime, e.g. a colour picker), and STRUCTURE.md (where components and styles go when feature work produces them). It ships ZERO COMPONENTS and zero opinionated visual styles; token VALUES are loudly-marked PLACEHOLDERS that exist only so the library compiles and the app runs on day zero, and are replaced wholesale by the design phase (bespunky-product-ux:stage-the-vision -> realize-the-vision). Also opens the sass channel and installs provideDesignSystem() on every Angular app (via design-system-styles). Workspace-level, always-on, idempotent: styles/ and src/ are SEEDED (written only if absent — a --repair must never restore placeholder tokens over the project's real design); config, tags, docs and app wiring are re-asserted every run.

design-system-styles.description: Wire ONE Angular app to the workspace's design system. (1) The SASS CHANNEL: adds the design system's parent dir to the app's build `stylePreprocessorOptions.includePaths`, so `@use 'design-system/styles' as ds;` resolves in-repo — necessary because the workspace links via tsconfig.base.json path aliases and SASS cannot read those, so a load path is the only channel; the specifier is deliberately the published one minus the npm scope. (2) CACHE CORRECTNESS: adds the design system to the app's `implicitDependencies`. The sass load path is not a graph edge on its own, so without an explicit dependency a token edit can leave `nx build <app>` replaying a CACHED bundle with the OLD tokens. This is deliberately an implicitDependencies edge (idiomatic; `nx affected` stays correct) — NOT a hand-rolled `inputs` array, which overrides the target's inferred inputs and hard-fails `nx build` on any workspace whose nx.json lacks the referenced named input (a real --repair hazard). The step-4 provider import usually creates the edge anyway; this guarantees it. (3) The `@use` + `@include ds.theme();` marker blocks in the app's global stylesheet (two blocks, at opposite ends, because sass rejects a `@use` that follows any other rule). (4) provideDesignSystem() in app.config.ts, so DsTheme is instantiated at app boot (and writes the mode attribute from boot) rather than from whenever a component first injects it — for the default 'system' mode there is no flash to prevent (the CSS resolves the OS preference on its own); a persisted explicit choice still needs an inline <head> script to beat first paint. Self-detecting (finds the design system by its `type:design-system` tag) and a clean no-op when there is none. Composed by BOTH the `design-system` generator (for apps that already exist) and the `app` generator (so a LATER app is wired with no flag and no recollection that this workspace has a design system) — one code path, both directions. Idempotent and --repair-safe: arrays merged and de-duplicated, stylesheet blocks upserted between their markers.

ds-theme.description: Add a THEME to the design system — a brand, a tenant palette, a user-selectable skin (`nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:ds-theme acme` -> packages/design-system/themes/acme.theme.scss, built to a standalone `theme-acme.css`). A theme is a CSS FILE of token overrides, NOT JavaScript, and that is the whole design: a `<link>` in <head> applies BEFORE first paint (a JS-applied theme lands after the bundle boots, so the user watches the default theme paint and then snap to the brand — a flash no service can fix); the browser caches and CDN-serves it for free; and because it is authored in SASS via `ds.theme-overrides()`, the design system's COMPILE-TIME guards survive — an unknown token name is a BUILD ERROR rather than a value that silently does nothing. Emits the same three-part structure the core theme does (`:root`, the per-mode blocks, AND the `prefers-color-scheme` blocks), so an override survives a mode switch and still applies for users on 'system' (the default — the most-missed case in hand-rolled theming). Registers the stylesheet on every Angular app's build with `inject: false` + a `bundleName` (Angular's own mechanism for a compiled-but-not-auto-linked stylesheet), which is what makes it swappable rather than always-on. Swap at runtime via `inject(DsRuntimeTheme).use('theme-acme.css')`. Idempotent: the theme source is seeded and never overwritten; the build wiring is re-asserted. A theme can only RE-BIND declared tokens — it cannot introduce new ones.

ds-component.description: Promote a reusable component INTO the design system, as its own SECONDARY ENTRY POINT (`nx g @bespunky/nx-tools:ds-component button` -> packages/design-system/button/, imported as `@<scope>/design-system/button`). One entry point per component — so each is independently tree-shakeable, is its own boundary, and no shared barrel becomes a merge-conflict hotspot. THIS IS THE ONLY WAY A COMPONENT ENTERS THE DESIGN SYSTEM: hand-creating the folder yields a component that resolves in the editor (through the path alias) and then VANISHES on publish, because the entry-point config IS the boundary. RUN IT ON THE SECOND OCCURRENCE of a UI pattern — the first card lives in the feature that needed it; the second time you do not copy it, you promote it, migrate BOTH sites, and delete the copies (two use-sites is exactly enough evidence to design the contract honestly; one is a guess, three is debt). Composes `secondary-entrypoint`, then adds the design-system specifics: the sass channel into the entry's own ng-package.json (so its SCSS can `@use` the token layer under ng-packagr), a starting SCSS in which every value is a TOKEN (making the right thing the easy thing at the exact moment a developer's first styling instinct lands), and an explicit public-API barrel (private members go in a `_`-prefixed folder named for what it holds — `_parts/`, `_material/` — and stay out of index.ts). Pairs with the bespunky-design-system:design-system-first skill, which carries the promotion discipline.

wiki-summary.description: Regenerate a library wiki's summary.json index (the compodoc `includes` SUMMARY). Devkit port of the legacy generate-wiki-summary Node script: walks the wiki folder via the Nx Tree (dry-run-safe, not raw fs), honoring per-folder .order files (split on /\r?\n/ — LF/CRLF-tolerant, fixing the old CRLF-only bug), building an ordered tree of { title, file, children? } where title = filename with dashes replaced by spaces and a folder gets children only when a sibling <folder>.md exists. Default wikiFolder packages/<lib>/wiki. Intended to also be wired as a Nx target in the docs build so summary.json regenerates in CI.
